concerns.text: FR-006 states 'System MUST apply statistical tests (Spearman correlation primary...) to assess significance of findings' but then specifies 'p-values are NOT reported for census data.' For a complete census, correlation coefficients are descriptive statistics, not test statistics. Cannot assess 'significance' without p-values or confidence intervals. Should rephrase to 'compute correlation coefficients as descriptive statistics' rather than 'apply statistical tests to assess significance'.

FR-013 frames KnotInfo validation as 'validation' while acknowledging databases may share underlying sources. When sources are shared, this is 'cross-checking for consistency, NOT independent verification' per spec. Should use more precise terminology throughout (e.g., 'data consistency check' instead of 'validation' when source independence cannot be established).

FR-005 acknowledges VIF will be high 'by design due to mathematical constraint (braid index ≤ crossing number)' but still proposes joint regression to assess 'joint explanatory power.' While not tautological (volume is independent from both predictors), the spec should clarify that this measures variance partitioning within the finite census, not independent predictive contributions. Currently stated in 'Variance Partitioning Clarification' but could be more prominent.
